title: Roasted Fermented Arabian Oysters Tagine
description: A bold, fermented tagine where shucked oysters are roasted in a fishsauce glaze and then simmered with cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, apricots, mint, and aromatic onions for a dense, showcaseworthy Middle Eastern seafood dish.

ingredients.name: Fermented fish sauce (or fermented fish brine)

instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (400 °F). Pat the oysters dry with a paper towel.

Whisk the fermented fish sauce with 1 tbsp of olive oil in a shallow dish. Dip each oyster in the mixture and arrange them on a parchment‑lined baking sheet. Roast for 10–12 minutes until the edges are just set.
While the oysters roast, heat the remaining 1 tbsp of olive oil in a large, heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Add cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika, cooking the spice blend for 30 seconds to bloom flavors.
Fold in the chopped apricots and mint, cooking for 2 minutes to soften.
Transfer the roasted oysters to the pot, pour in the fresh lemon juice, then sprinkle salt and pepper over the top.
Cover, reduce heat to low, and let the tagine simmer gently for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed; serve hot, optionally garnished with toasted almonds or fresh mint leaves.
